completion_data.completion_summary: Resynced root README.md Module Map, completed docs/README.md index with 6 missing entries plus a new docs-verified-against-source convention, created modules/README.md documenting the system/home split and verified health notes, added a task-69 dual-home-manager closure confirming note, folded in two optional single-line drift fixes, and passed a full-regression build harness (nix flake check + nandi/hamsa/garuda builds + HM activation) with zero residual README-vs-find drift.

memory_candidates.content: When a doc-only capstone task resyncs README-style docs against a moved tree, always re-verify every cited line number against the CURRENT tree before editing (grep -n on the exact strings) rather than trusting a prior research report's line numbers verbatim — the report itself should have done this, but a second verification pass at implement time catches drift introduced between research and implementation.

For a doc-only task with sibling tasks having in-flight uncommitted changes in the same specs/ tree, commit per-phase with explicit `git add <paths>` naming only this task's own files (never `git add -A`), and verify with `git diff --staged --name-only` before each commit. This kept 6+ unrelated sibling-task edits (specs/069, 086, 087, 088, 089, 093, TODO.md, state.json) untouched throughout task 91's implementation.

A doc-only task's Non-Goals/optional-phase scoping should be honored literally even when adjacent stale content in the same file would make more sense to fix together — record the adjacent staleness as an explicit follow-up note (not silently fixed, not silently ignored) rather than expanding scope. Example: task 91 fixed exactly docs/configuration.md:20 and docs/unstable-packages.md:12 per explicit instruction, while flagging adjacent stale task-66 references in the same files (lines 18-19/22-24 and 5-7 respectively) as a recorded follow-up.
